sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports intermittent shortness of breath, particularly when near his cat or outdoors, and occasional eye irritation. He describes a history of frequent diarrhea when traveling and mentions a nebulizer prescribed after an urgent care visit. He denies current symptoms of travel-related nausea or vomiting.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Physical exam reveals faint expiratory wheezing bilaterally in all lung fields. Pulse oximetry is normal. No rash is observed. No stridor is audible.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: Newly diagnosed allergic asthma is suspected. Allergy testing is planned to identify potential triggers.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: Rescribe albuterol inhaler for immediate symptom relief. Prescribe singulair 10mg daily to reduce asthma occurrences. Schedule allergy testing and follow-up in one week.
